title: ulmus minor subsp. cornubiensis
intro: Ulmus minor subsp. cornubiensis, commonly known as the Cornish elm or dwarf elm, is a subspecies of the small-leaved elm tree native to the southwestern tip of England, particularly in Cornwall. The medicinal properties of Ulmus minor subsp. cornubiensis lie in its bark, which contains a high concentration of tannins, making it a natural astringent and anti-inflammatory. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Ulmus minor subsp. cornubiensis is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
